text:
HEMATOPATHOLOGY REPORT
Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine
Hematopathology Division

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PATIENT: Kowalczyk, Renata
DOB: [on file]
SEX: Female
MRN: [on file]
ACCESSION #: HP-[XXXX]-XXXXXX
ORDERING PHYSICIAN: Hematology/Oncology Attending
CLINICAL INDICATION: New leukocytosis (WBC 87,400/µL), anemia (Hgb 7.2 g/dL), thrombocytopenia (platelets 34,000/µL), and circulating blasts on peripheral smear; rule out acute leukemia.
PROCEDURE DATE: [Date on file]
DATE RECEIVED: [Date on file]
DATE REPORTED: [Date on file]
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

SPECIMEN(S) SUBMITTED:
  A. Bone marrow core biopsy, posterior iliac crest (right)
  B. Bone marrow aspirate smears, posterior iliac crest (right) (6 smears submitted, May-Grünwald-Giemsa stained)
  C. Bone marrow aspirate, clot section
  D. Peripheral blood smear (submitted for concurrent differential review)

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
FINAL DIAGNOSIS
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

A–C. BONE MARROW BIOPSY AND ASPIRATE, POSTERIOR ILIAC CREST:

  ACUTE MYELOID LEUKEMIA (AML), WITH MONOCYTIC DIFFERENTIATION
  (FAB CLASSIFICATION: M4, ACUTE MYELOMONOCYTIC LEUKEMIA)
  -- De novo; no prior history of myelodysplastic syndrome or cytotoxic therapy
  -- Blast count: 74% of nucleated marrow cells by aspirate differential (exceeds 20% diagnostic threshold)
  -- Immunophenotype consistent with AML with myeloid and monocytic differentiation (see ancillary studies)
  -- Markedly hypercellular marrow (>95% cellularity)
  -- Auer rods identified (rare)
  -- FLT3-ITD MUTATION DETECTED (fragment analysis, see molecular report)
  -- DNMT3A R882H CO-MUTATION DETECTED (reflex molecular testing)
  -- Normal female karyotype: 46,XX [20/20] (see cytogenetics report)

D. PERIPHERAL BLOOD SMEAR:
  Marked leukocytosis with numerous circulating blasts and immature monocytic forms; rare Auer rods. Normocytic anemia. Severe thrombocytopenia. See comment.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
GROSS DESCRIPTION
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Specimen A: Received in formalin is a bone marrow core biopsy measuring 1.6 cm in length x 0.2 cm in diameter. The specimen is gray-white, firm, and continuous. The entire specimen is submitted in one cassette following decalcification.

Specimen B: Received are six bone marrow aspirate smears on glass slides, labeled with patient name and MRN. The smears are of adequate cellularity with visible particles. All slides are examined after May-Grünwald-Giemsa (MGG) staining.

Specimen C: Received in formalin is a bone marrow aspirate clot section, measuring approximately 0.5 cm in greatest dimension; gray-white, soft. The entire specimen is submitted in one cassette.

Specimen D: Received are two peripheral blood smear slides, labeled appropriately. Smears examined after Wright-Giemsa staining.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

BONE MARROW CORE BIOPSY (Specimen A):
The core biopsy is adequate for evaluation (1.6 cm, >10 evaluable intertrabecular spaces). The marrow is markedly hypercellular, estimated at greater than 95% cellularity, with near-complete replacement of normal adipose tissue by hematopoietic and neoplastic elements. The normal marrow architecture is largely effaced. The predominant population consists of medium to large immature cells with high nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratios, irregular nuclear contours, folded or indented nuclei, open chromatin, and variably prominent nucleoli — morphologically consistent with myeloblasts. Interspersed is a second population of larger cells with more abundant pale-to-granular cytoplasm, round-to-kidney-shaped nuclei, and fine chromatin, consistent with promonocytes and monocytic blasts. Residual trilineage hematopoiesis is markedly diminished. Megakaryocytes are markedly reduced in number; occasional dyspoietic megakaryocytes with hypolobated nuclei are noted, though the degree of dysplasia does not reach the threshold for myelodysplasia-related changes as a primary diagnosis. Bone trabeculae appear normal without osteolytic changes.

BONE MARROW ASPIRATE SMEARS (Specimen B) — 500-cell differential count performed:
  - Blasts (myeloblasts): 51%
  - Promonocytes/monocytic blasts: 23% (total blast equivalent: 74%)
  - Mature monocytes: 4%
  - Promyelocytes: 2%
  - Myelocytes: 2%
  - Metamyelocytes: 3%
  - Band/segmented neutrophils: 5%
  - Eosinophils: 1%
  - Erythroid precursors: 6%
  - Lymphocytes: 2%
  - Plasma cells: <1%

Blast morphology: The myeloblasts are medium to large, measuring approximately 15–20 µm in diameter, with a high nuclear-to-cytoplasmic (N:C) ratio. Nuclei are oval to irregular, with folded or reniform contours in a subset of cells. Chromatin is fine and stippled with one to three prominent nucleoli. The cytoplasm is scant to moderate, faintly basophilic, and occasionally contains fine azurophilic granulation. Rare Auer rods are identified (thin, rod-shaped, azurophilic intracytoplasmic inclusions seen in 3–4 blasts per 100x high-power field). The monocytic component (promonocytes) features cells with abundant pale gray-to-basophilic cytoplasm, irregular nuclear contours, delicate chromatin, and inconspicuous to small nucleoli. These promonocytes are counted as blast equivalents per WHO 2022 criteria.

Aspiration quality: Particles are present; spicules examined confirm marrow hypercellularity. Iron stores assessed on Prussian blue-stained smear: Markedly decreased storage iron (grade 0–1); no ringed sideroblasts identified.

CLOT SECTION (Specimen C): Confirms hypercellularity with sheets of blasts consistent with findings in the core biopsy.

PERIPHERAL BLOOD SMEAR (Specimen D):
Marked leukocytosis. The differential reveals a predominance of blasts and immature monocytic forms comprising approximately 58% of the differential. Blasts are morphologically similar to those described in the marrow. Rare Auer rods are identified in circulating blasts. Remaining white cells include bands, segmented neutrophils, monocytes, and rare eosinophils. Red blood cells show normocytic, normochromic morphology with mild anisopoikilocytosis. Platelets are markedly reduced; occasional large platelets noted.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ANCILLARY STUDIES
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (performed on core biopsy sections; B5-fixed, decalcified):

   Marker           Result
   ───────────────────────────────────
   CD34             Positive (diffuse, strong; marks blasts)
   CD117 (c-Kit)    Positive (diffuse, strong; marks blasts)
   CD33             Positive (diffuse)
   CD13             Positive (diffuse)
   MPO (myeloperoxidase) Focally positive (positive in myeloblast component; reduced/negative in monocytic component)
   CD64             Positive (marks monocytic component)
   CD14             Partially positive (subset of monocytic cells)
   CD68 (PGM1)      Positive (monocytic component)
   TdT              Negative (excludes lymphoblastic component)
   CD3              Negative
   CD20             Negative
   CD19             Negative
   PAX5             Negative
   Lysozyme         Positive (monocytic component)
   Ki-67 proliferation index: Approximately 78% (markedly elevated, consistent with high-grade leukemia)

   Interpretation: The immunophenotype supports AML with both myeloid (CD34+, CD117+, MPO+, CD13+, CD33+) and monocytic (CD64+, CD14+/-, CD68+, lysozyme+) differentiation, consistent with FAB M4 (acute myelomonocytic leukemia).

2. FLOW CYTOMETRY IMMUNOPHENOTYPING (bone marrow aspirate; 8-color multiparameter panel):

   An abnormal blast population comprising approximately 72% of viable events is identified with the following immunophenotype:

   Positive:    CD45(dim), CD34, CD117, HLA-DR, CD33 (bright), CD13, CD64 (bright), CD11b (partial), CD11c (partial), CD38, CD123
   Partial/dim: CD14 (subset ~30% of blast gate), CD15 (dim, subset)
   Negative:    CD3, CD4 (within blast gate), CD5, CD7, CD10, CD19, CD20, CD56, TdT, CD16, CD41, CD61
   MPO:         Positive (cytoplasmic; approximately 65% of blast gate)

   Interpretation: Immunophenotype is consistent with acute myeloid leukemia with monocytic differentiation. The CD45(dim)/CD34+/CD117+/HLA-DR+/CD64+ bright immunophenotype is characteristic of AML with monocytic component (FAB M4/M5 spectrum). CD14 partial positivity and bright CD64 further support the monocytic differentiation. No lymphoid antigen aberrancy is detected. This immunophenotype will serve as the leukemia-associated immunophenotype (LAIP) for future MRD monitoring.

3. CYTOCHEMISTRY (aspirate smears):

   Myeloperoxidase (MPO) stain:
     Positive: Myeloblast component (>3% blasts MPO-positive; confirms myeloid lineage)
     Negative/weakly positive: Monocytic component (as expected)

   Non-specific esterase (NSE; alpha-naphthyl butyrate esterase):
     Positive: Monocytic blast/promonocyte component (strong, diffuse; inhibited by sodium fluoride — confirmatory for monocytic lineage)
     Negative to weak: Myeloblast component

   Chloroacetate esterase (CAE; specific esterase):
     Positive: Myeloid component

   Prussian blue (iron stain):
     Decreased iron stores; no ringed sideroblasts

   Sudan black B: Positive in myeloid component (correlates with MPO)

4. CYTOGENETICS (conventional G-banding karyotype, bone marrow aspirate):

   Result: 46,XX [20/20]
   Interpretation: Normal female karyotype. No cytogenetic abnormalities detected. Twenty metaphases analyzed; all normal.
   Note: Absence of t(8;21)(q22;q22.1), inv(16)(p13.1q22), or t(15;17)(q24.1;q21.2) confirmed; no AML-defining cytogenetic aberrancies. This patient's AML is molecularly defined (FLT3-ITD), not cytogenetically defined.

5. MOLECULAR / FLUORESCENCE IN SITU HYBRIDIZATION (FISH) — RESULTS REPORTED SEPARATELY; SUMMARY PROVIDED FOR COMPLETENESS:

   FLT3-ITD (fragment analysis): DETECTED — insertion length 48 bp, allelic ratio 0.82 (high allelic ratio, VAF ~82%) [FINAL]
   DNMT3A R882H (PCR/Sanger sequencing): DETECTED — VAF approximately 47% [FINAL]
   NPM1 exon 12 mutation: NOT DETECTED [FINAL]
   CEBPA (biallelic): NOT DETECTED [FINAL]
   IDH1 R132: NOT DETECTED [FINAL]
   IDH2 R140/R172: NOT DETECTED [FINAL]

   AML-Defining Gene Fusions (RT-PCR reflex panel): [PENDING — results to follow]
     PML-RARA [t(15;17)]: Pending
     RUNX1-RUNX1T1 [t(8;21)]: Pending
     CBFB-MYH11 [inv(16)/t(16;16)]: Pending
     BCR-ABL1 [t(9;22)]: Pending
     KMT2A (MLL) rearrangement: Pending
     DEK-NUP214: Pending

   Comprehensive Hematologic Malignancy NGS Panel (523-gene): [PENDING — see separate NGS report when resulted]

   Note: Pending molecular results will be addended to this report upon receipt.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ADDENDUM (to be completed upon receipt of pending molecular results)
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  AML-defining gene fusions (RT-PCR panel): ALL NEGATIVE — PML-RARA negative, RUNX1-RUNX1T1 negative, CBFB-MYH11 negative, BCR-ABL1 negative, KMT2A rearrangement negative, DEK-NUP214 negative. [ADDENDED]

  Comprehensive NGS panel: See separate NGS report. Summary: FLT3-ITD confirmed (high AR, VAF 82%); DNMT3A R882H confirmed (VAF 47%); no additional clinically actionable mutations; normal karyotype; no pathogenic copy number alterations. [ADDENDED]

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
COMMENT
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The morphologic, immunohistochemical, flow cytometric, cytochemical, and molecular findings are integrated and diagnostic of de novo acute myeloid leukemia (AML) with monocytic differentiation, FAB M4 (acute myelomonocytic leukemia), WHO 2022 category "AML, NOS" with monocytic differentiation, with a concurrent FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio mutation and DNMT3A R882H co-mutation.

The blast count of 74% (by WHO 2022 criteria, combining myeloblasts and promonocytes as blast equivalents) substantially exceeds the 20% threshold required for diagnosis of AML, and is consistent with the presenting peripheral blood findings of marked leukocytosis (WBC 87,400/µL) with circulating blasts.

The identification of rare Auer rods confirms myeloid lineage and is consistent with FAB M4 morphology; notably, Auer rods can be seen in both myeloblasts and occasionally in abnormal promyelocytes in AML but were not observed in the monocytic component in this case.

The combined myeloperoxidase positivity (myeloid component) and non-specific esterase positivity (monocytic component) by cytochemistry, confirmed by immunophenotyping (CD64 bright, CD14 partial), is the hallmark of acute myelomonocytic leukemia (FAB M4). This is in contrast to FAB M5 (pure monocytic/monoblastic AML), in which the monocytic component would predominate (>80% blasts of monocytic lineage).

The normal karyotype (46,XX) indicates this is not a core-binding factor AML (no t(8;21) or inv(16)) and not acute promyelocytic leukemia (no t(15;17)), which are distinguished by their specific morphologic, immunophenotypic, and molecular features. In the absence of AML-defining chromosomal abnormalities and in the context of the identified FLT3-ITD mutation with high allelic ratio (>0.5) without NPM1 co-mutation, this case is classified as ELN 2022 Adverse risk.

There is no morphologic or cytogenetic evidence of a pre-existing myelodysplastic syndrome or therapy-related process. The diagnosis is de novo AML.

The DNMT3A R882H mutation (VAF ~47%), detected at approximately 50% variant allele frequency, is consistent with a founding clonal event and is commonly co-mutated with FLT3-ITD in de novo AML. DNMT3A mutations at R882 are known to impair DNMT3A tetramerization and reduce enzymatic activity, resulting in focal DNA hypomethylation. While also a recognized clonal hematopoiesis of indeterminate potential (CHIP) driver gene, in the current context (74% blasts, high VAF), this mutation is considered part of the leukemic clone rather than pre-existing CHIP.

The LAIP (leukemia-associated immunophenotype) established by flow cytometry — CD45dim/CD34+/CD117+/HLA-DR+/CD64bright/CD33bright/MPO+ — will serve as the reference immunophenotype for subsequent minimal residual disease (MRD) monitoring by multiparameter flow cytometry following therapy.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DIAGNOSING PATHOLOGIST: Attending Hematopathologist
CASE REVIEWED BY: Hematopathology Fellow (contributing)
E-SIGNED: [Date/Time on file]
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
